description: Unit (table-driven): NinjaOne normalizer maps TRIGGERED/RESET/ACKNOWLEDGED payloads, normalizes all severity values, derives condition identity from statusCode with activityType fallback
Unit: TacticalRMM payload maps to a NormalizedRmmAlertEvent
Unit (table-driven): rule condition matrix — each condition type matches and mismatches, multi-condition rules require all fields, empty conditions is catch-all
Unit: rule selection — lower priority_order wins, inactive rules skipped, other integrations'/tenants' rules never evaluated
Unit: a rule with an invalid stored regex is logged, skipped, and the next rule is evaluated
Unit: dedup_key computation from device + condition identity
Unit: ticket templates render all placeholders; defaults apply when no template
Unit (table-driven): untouched determination — human comment, time entry, manual status change each count as touched; rule auto-assignment does not

Unit (table-driven): window matching — one-off in/out of range, weekly recurrence in window timezone including across midnight, scope combinations require all non-null scopes, inactive windows never match
Unit: shared Zod schemas reject malformed conditions/actions shapes and invalid messagePattern regex

Integration: TRIGGERED webhook end-to-end — rmm_alerts row with metadata/dedup_key/matched_rule_id, ticket honoring boardId/priorityOverride/assignToUserId with source, source_reference, asset association, and initial comment; auto_ticket_created set
Integration: replaying the same webhook creates no duplicate alert row and no duplicate ticket
Integration: repeat trigger while ticket open — occurrence_count incremented, re-triggered comment added, no new ticket
Integration: trigger after linked ticket closed creates a new ticket; same condition on a different device creates a separate ticket
Integration: no matching rule, or createTicket false, stores the alert without a ticket
Integration: severity-to-priority fallback applies when the rule has no priorityOverride
Integration: ticket client resolves from the asset, falling back to the organization mapping
Integration: RESET resolves the alert, comments the ticket, closes untouched tickets via autoResolveStatusId or the default closed status
Integration: RESET leaves touched tickets open (comment only); without autoResolveTicket the ticket is untouched entirely
Integration: acknowledged events set status acknowledged with acknowledged_at
Integration: ticket close calls the outbound adapter when resetAlertOnTicketClose is true; skips when the rule opted out or the alert is already resolved
Integration: outbound reset failure is logged and stamped in metadata without blocking the close; providers without an adapter are skipped cleanly
Integration: alert in a matching window is stored suppressed with suppressed_by_window_id — no ticket, no notification, no workflow event; a reset for it resolves quietly
Integration: poll cycle upserts a missed RMM-active alert into a ticket, synthesizes a reset for a stale local active alert, and processes an expired-window suppressed alert into a ticket
Integration: the same alert arriving via webhook and poller yields one alert row and one ticket
Integration: rule CRUD validates shapes and regex at save, requires admin, and is tenant-isolated
Integration: window CRUD validates shapes, requires admin, and is tenant-isolated
Integration: pipeline publishes RMM_ALERT_TRIGGERED and RMM_ALERT_RESOLVED workflow v2 events with alert, asset/client, and ticket IDs
Integration: rmm.alerts.create_ticket workflow action creates a ticket for an unlinked alert and errors cleanly when already linked
Integration: OAuth callback rejects a wrong or expired csrf state and accepts a valid one
Integration: matched rule notifyUserIds produces in-app notifications honoring the rmm-alert category
Integration: an org mapping with auto_create_tickets false still gets tickets when a rule matches (flag no longer consulted)
